content.question: What is the quantified causal contribution of the 6/8-year gimnázium track system itself to Hungary's EU-leading school segregation, net of other mechanisms such as residential sorting, church-school expansion, selective commuting ('szelektív ingázás'), and free school choice?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a quasi-experimental analysis using administrative data on school catchment areas, track applications, and feeder primary-school composition changes to isolate the track-specific segregation effect. Compare segregation trends in municipalities with versus without local 6/8-year track availability, controlling for demographic and residential factors.
